observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Require the Municipal Development Council to propose feasible priority projects that can be implemented within the year, ensuring timely delivery of benefits to the target beneficiaries;
2. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), the Municipal Engineer and other concerned LGU officials to closely monitor the implementation of priority projects; and
3. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement process and direct the Municipal Engineer and other project proponents to implement immediately all unimplemented PPAs embodied in the approved Annual/Supplemental Investment Plan in order to attain optimal socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
observations.managementAction: 1. Require the priority development projects that can be implemented within the year to be proposed; 2. Require MPDO, Municipal Engineer and other concerned LGU officials to closely monitor the implementation of priority projects; 3. Require Bids and Awards Committee to expedite procurement process. A letter was sent to MRS. DIRMA T. FRIAS of COA, Virac, explaining in detail the reason for the delay of implementation of priority projects dated May 22, 2025 (hereto attach marked as ANNEX A). The MPDO proposed feasible priority projects that can be implemented within the year. Procurement and implementation actions are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: 1. Instruct the Accounting and Budget Office to reconcile their records on allotments and obligations periodically so that any resulting differences can be immediately verified and corrected.
2. Reconcile the records regarding the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the BIR.
3. Reconcile the records regarding the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the Pag-IBIG.
4. Revert to the GF the unexpended balance of LDRRMF beyond the five-year period, to be made available for other social services, in conformity with Section 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
5. Issue ICS on tangible property and ARE for PPE for existing property and future purchases.

Submit the following required supporting documents for the repair of various equipment and motor vehicles totaling ₱373,568.28 and for future identical disbursements:

6. Pre-repair inspection reports;

7. Waste material reports; and

8. Post-inspection reports.
9. Cause the reconciliation of the records and properly transfer the unspent prior years’ 20% DF from the General Fund account to the 20% DF account pursuant to the Section 313 of RA No. 7160.
10. Require the barangay to record the payable account and provide updates for the immediate liquidation of the subject fund transfer upon project completion.

12. Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost as a result of delayed implementation. Also, re-evaluate the necessity of the projects under the continuing appropriation and consider the realignment of insignificant projects.

13. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the BAC Resolution for adopting the use of small value procurement as an alternative mode of procurement, Purchase Request, distribution list duly signed by the beneficiaries signifying their receipt of items and delivery receipt for the items received

19. Enact a resolution to open a separate depository account for the 20% DF, transfer the unspent fund and regularly transfer the 20% IRA from the General Fund account to this account pursuant to the Section 313 of RA No. 7160.

The Municipal Treasurer requires all revenue collecting officers to remit religiously their collections on a daily basis, or in the case of the collectors assigned in the field, where travel time from places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as their collections reach P5,000.00.

Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I; 

Collectors issue in chronological order the pre-numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence; and

Management impose disciplinary actions or sanctions when necessary, in order to enforce compliance with the existing rules and regulations on collection, reporting and remittances and in order to safeguard government funds.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.  Likewise, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made, to ensure reliability and accuracy of the recorded financial transactions.

The LDRRMO review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund and submit the same to the audit team; and

Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statement and accounting record the particular year to which the balances of STF-DRRM were unexpended for proper monitoring and budgeting and any unutilized balance aged more than five years shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

Direct the GAD Focal Person to expedite the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to appropriately identify gender issues and provide basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and

Direct the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to conduct annual monitoring of the accomplishments and evaluation of the utilization and outcome of the GAD budget and prepare the form prescribed in Item H of the JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended and the Management agreed to appropriate only those programs/projects/activities enumerated under DepEd-DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 for Special Education Fund and avoid charging unrelated expenses which could defeat the purpose for which the fund was created.

The Local Chief Executive ensure the timely submission of properly accomplished action plan on the CY 2023 AAR findings and recommendations including that of the prior years and submit the same to the audit team; and 

Hereafter, the Management shall faithfully observe the 60-day period within which to submit to the audit team from receipt of the succeeding AARs, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

The Accounting and the Property Units undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled in order to come up with a duly reconciled PPE balances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to review the JEVs prepared to identify the causes of the discrepancies and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.  Likewise, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made, to ensure that all transactions are properly and accurately recorded in the books of accounts.

Exert concerted efforts to intensify collections of Real Property Tax including the interest thereon by developing, designing and implementing collection strategies, so that more development programs may be implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and

Direct the LDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.  Henceforth, review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan to incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years to facilitate effective monitoring of the fund.

The Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels shall be submitted to the Auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.

That the materials of the project which were not found on site should be delivered and the project be implemented as intended, otherwise the total cost of the materials and labor should be refunded immediately.  Failure to do so shall cause this office to apply legal remedies to recover the said amount.

The Solid Waste Management Office continuously coordinate with the concerned Barangays to prioritize and facilitate the establishment of functional MRFs as required under section 32 of Republic Act No. 9003 to help achieve the Municipality’s commitment towards sustainable development and implementation of an environmentally sound and safe management of its solid waste.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Determine an appropriate useful life and recognize depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for all PPEs, including those intended to be transferred to different barangays in the Municipality;

Review the computation of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for all depreciable PPE accounts following the prescribed procedures provided by IPSAS No. 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Management:

Submit copies of debit and credit memos that support transactions processed by the bank for verification in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Provide a sufficient description of the reconciling items on the face of the BRS or in any other supporting document or schedule to facilitate verification of the accuracy and legitimacy of the reconciling items.

We recommended that the Management  ensure that all necessary documents and required certifications for procurement be prepared and submitted in adherence to Section 86 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Management submit the duly accomplished AAPSI Form on the audit observations and recommendations within sixty (60) days from receipt of the AAR to the Office of the Auditor. Moreover, all items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor order the: a) GSO-Designate to maintain and update regarding acquisition, description, custody, disposal, and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions and reconcile with the Accounting Records. b) Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant: i. to complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts. ii. Prepare a report on the result of the physical count through RPCPPE including list of unserviceable PPEs, missing PPEs, and items found at station. iii. If accountability/responsibility over the missing PPE is determined after investigation, demand shall be made from the accountable personnel to produce the item or to pay the depreciated replacement cost thereof. If the missing PPE cannot be determined, make a justification indicating therein the procedures conducted. iv. to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: a) GSO-Designate to maintain and update regarding acquisition, description, custody, disposal, and other information of PPE based on source documents of the transactions and reconcile with the Accounting Records. b) Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant: i. to complete the physical inventory within prescribed period for one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts. ii. Prepare a report on the result of the physical count through RPCPPE including list of unserviceable PPEs, missing PPEs, and items found at station. iii. Any discrepancy between the RPCPPE, between the PPELCs, and PCs shall be investigated, verified, and adjusted. The total reconciled balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance of PPE at year-end; and; iv. to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.reason: Implemented Discrepancy of P45,491.60 between RPCPPE and Ledger still exist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the depreciation of the infrastructure assets from the date of completion as indicated in the Certificate of Completion, and for the machinery and equipment, from the date of delivery as indicated in the Acceptance and Inspection Report. The depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant draw adjusting entries to recognize the depreciation of the PPE items not subjected to depreciation in CY 2023 and prior years.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to recognize the depreciation of assets from date of completion/date of delivery and to draw adjusting journal entries to recognize the depreciation for prior years' items not subjected to depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a) The Supply Officer to: i) prepare an Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for items costing below P15,000.00, and Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for items costing P15,000.00 and above upon its issuance to end-user; ii) use the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) to record all supplies issued; iii) prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued based on the RIS; and iv) submit the RSMI, duly supported with all RIS, to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts. b) The Municipal Accountant to: i) reclassify the supplies for stockpiling from the Inventory Held for Consumption to the appropriate account under the Inventory Held for Distribution; and ii) prepare the appropriate Journal Entry Voucher to record the issued supplies to the end-users based on the RSMI and RIS of the Supply Officer.
observations.managementAction: Supply Officer to prepare ICS/PAR, use RIS, prepare RSMI and submit to Municipal Accountant; Municipal Accountant to reclassify supplies and prepare JEVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC Chairperson, undertake the following: a. Require the submission of the latest Business Tax Return within five (5) calendar days from receipt by the bidder of the notice from the BAC that the bidder has the Lowest Calculated Bid or Highest Rated Bid. b. Carefully verify, validate, and ascertain all statements made in the documents submitted by the bidder, and shall immediately notify the said bidder in writing of its post-disqualification and the grounds for it, if any, and only declare bidders as eligible if they strictly adhere to the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184. c. Submit justification why the contractor was awarded with four (4) new contracts despite having negative slippage of its on-going and previous contracts with the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Require submission of latest Business Tax Return; validate documents; submit justification for awards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor impose liquidated damages of at least one-tenth (1/10) of one (1) percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay, and ensure that the time extension granted is reasonable. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer: a. determine the reasonableness of the contractor's requests for time extension by examining supporting relevant documents prior to recommending its approval; b. submit to the Audit Team supporting documents to justify the grant of time extension due to unfavorable weather conditions; c. provide for the computation of the additional liquidated damages to be imposed for the projects; d. monitor the performance of the contractors to ensure timely implementation; e. include the pre-determined rainy/unworkable days in the computation of the original contract time during the conduct of a detailed engineering plan.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer to examine supporting documents, submit to Audit Team, compute liquidated damages, monitor contractor performance, include pre-determined rainy/unworkable days.
observations.reason: Not implemented. Implemented: Municipal Accountant prepared a Demand Letter dated May 20, 2024. However, the demand letter was not yet issued to the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Accountant to disclose all relevant information pertaining the available, utilized and balances of the current year and Special Trust Fund of Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements. b) MDRRMO to ensure that all PPAs charged against all sources of the LDRRMF are incorporated in the LDRRMFIP or Revised LDRRMFIP. c) MPDC to prepare a supplemental Annual Investment Program based on the LDRRMFIP or Revised LDRRMFIP, and secure approval of the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the commencement of project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to disclose details; MDRRMO to ensure PPAs included in LDRRMFIP; MPDC to prepare supplemental AIP and secure approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. require the deputy collectors to remit all collections daily and intact to the Municipal Treasurer; and b. issue a written authority deputizing the Barangay Treasurers to collect Community Tax on her behalf. In the absence of said authority, require the collectors to remit collections and accountable forms to the Municipal Treasurer. Henceforth, no accountable forms shall be issued to Barangay Treasurers without a written authority as deputy collectors.
observations.managementAction: Issue written deputation to Barangay Treasurers or require immediate remittance of collections and accountable forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Peace and Order Councils to include only those PPAs that are directly related to crime prevention and law enforcement activities, capability development for personnel of law enforcement agencies, volunteers/partners, or programs for anti-illegal drug, illegal gambling, counter-insurgency, and/or counter-terrorism efforts, among others that will directly contribute to the maintenance of the peace and order and public safety. We recommended MPDC to, in the preparation of the AIP, conduct a thorough review of the Peace and Order and Public Safety (POPS) Plan of the LGU and ensure that all proposed PPAs are aligned with the priorities outlined in the POPS Plan and duly supported by the Peace and Order Plan.
observations.managementAction: Include only PPAs directly related to peace and order; MPDC to review and align AIP with POPS Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Health Officer: i) issue a Special Order covering the assignment of public health workers to hazardous/difficult areas, ii) secure Certification by the Secretary of DOH that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-torn / embattled / disease-infested /distress or isolated areas, or areas declared under state of calamity or emergency, or with volcano activity or eruption, and iii) attach the complete supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers. We likewise recommended the Municipal Accountant certify disbursement vouchers that are supported with complete documentation and strictly observe the guidelines on the grant of hazard pay, subsistence and laundry allowances.
observations.managementAction: Issue Special Orders and secure DOH certifications; Municipal Accountant to certify supporting docs before payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the Municipal Treasurer return the unexpended fund transfers to their respective source agencies and immediate reversion of the Congressional Funds that was unconstitutional by the Supreme Court; b) the Accountant prepare the aging of fund transfers made using the format required in Item 7.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and provide disclosures on dormant balances, if any, in the Notes to Financial Statements; c) request for write-off of the dormant fund transfers, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; d) The Municipal Treasurer to refund/remit to the source agencies or the Bureau of Treasury the unexpended balance after the completion of the purpose of fund transfers; e) Submit to the Municipal Accountant the proof of receipt of fund transfers and refund of unexpended balance for its attachment to the liquidation report.
observations.managementAction: Prepare aging of fund transfers and disclosures; request write-offs if warranted; refund unexpended balances to source agencies.
observations.reason: Implemented: Representation with source agencies made to reconcile unliquidated fund transfer. No request for write-off submitted to audit team. No return of balances made in CY 2024 due to absence of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the: a. The MDRRM Officer to prepare an LDRRMFIP to incorporate prioritized PPAs and updated total resource requirements for PPAs not included in annual budget, for approval by the DRRM Council; b. MPDC to prepare a supplemental AIP to incorporate the new prioritized PPAs and resource requirements based on the approved LDRRMFIP and secure approval from the Sangguniang Bayan; c. The Municipal Treasurer to revert the unexpended balances if any.
observations.managementAction: Prepare LDRRMFIP and supplemental AIP; revert unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a) the Municipal Accountant to disclose all relevant information on donations in the Notes to Financial Statements and maintain CDUR and RCDU; b) MDRRMO to ensure donors' funds are incorporated in LDRRMFIP and AIP; c) ensure transparency and proper monitoring of donations.
observations.managementAction: Disclose donations in Notes; maintain CDUR and RCDU; incorporate donations into LDRRMFIP/AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the GSO to maintain records regarding acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information of PPE based on the source documents of the transactions and reconcile with the Accounting Records. We also recommended Inventory Committee to follow guidelines in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for Physical Count, Recognition of PPE Found at Station, and Disposition of Non-existing/Missing PPE items for one-time cleansing.
observations.managementAction: GSO to maintain and update records; Inventory Committee to follow COA Circular No. 2020-006 and undertake disposal.
observations.reason: Disposal was not initiated in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Make representation with the bank to ask for alternative remedy and exhaust all other means to correct the error made by the bank; b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the bank and submit necessary documents to support the correction of bank errors.
observations.managementAction: Make representation with the bank and submit necessary documents to support correction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace prior years' accounting entries, locate causes of negative balances and initiate corrections/adjustments either to request for payment for employees for over-deductions of salaries or refund the overpayment, whatever appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Trace prior years' accounting entries and perform thorough review to ascertain cause and correct.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct MDRRMO to incorporate disbursements into LDRRMFIP and ensure propriety; MPDC to prepare supplemental AIP for prioritized PPAs and secure approval; ensure funded projects are included in budget.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO to prepare LDRRMFIP and MPDC to prepare supplemental AIP and secure approvals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the bank and submit the necessary documents to support the correction of the bank error; also to reconcile balances and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements.
observations.managementAction: Made representation with bank and submitted necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review the noted observations above and submit justification letter citing the reason for unreconciled balances between the reciprocal accounts of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, non-recording of depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation for PPEs and inconsistent figures in the FS, Notes to FS and supporting schedules.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: No action was provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 percent DF in the succeeding period by including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready. Also, lessen frequent changes in the planned PPAs to ensure immediate implementation and completion within the target date.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Exert more effort to identify the sources of the unreconciled difference between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds through reconciliation of the accounts; and b. After which, if the issue remained unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on write-off of receivables and/or payables upon presentation of the required documentation.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken.
observations.reason: No action was provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare individual financial statements with complete subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise operated by the Municipality and for its 20% Development Fund which should form part of the schedules of the General Fund in accordance with the above cited regulations; and b. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Head of the economic enterprise to continuously appropriate different funds for Waterworks and Cemetery but separate the appropriation for Market and Slaughterhouse in the ensuing year to facilitate the reconciliation with the records of the Accounting office.
observations.managementAction: No action taken. The Municipality indicated that they will maintain separate accounting and appropriate funds in succeeding years. Some funds for Waterworks and Cemetery were separated in subsequent budgets.
observations.reason: No action was provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze and reconcile non-moving balances and other related accounts to determine their proper disposition. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a Resolution requesting the proper write-off of the Receivables from KKK proponents and submit it to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the cited provision. Likewise, submit a copy of the proof of efforts exerted by the Municipality to recover the said amount.
observations.managementAction: No action had been taken yet by the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the non-moving balances. The Municipal office indicated that they will follow up and coordinate with concerned offices for proper documentation and possible write-off procedures.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and MA to strictly comply with Item 2.6(a), Chapter 2, Part II of the BOM for LGUs in the determination of funds available for supplemental budgets.
observations.reason: Audit observation was reiterated under Observation Nos. 18 and 19, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the MA, accountable officers and authorized officials to submit within 10 days after the end of the month all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents as required under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 for the conduct of a timely and effective auditorial review and evaluation of the agency’s transactions. We also recommended that the LCE ensure strict monitoring of compliance of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The MA committed to fast-track submission of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and its attachments is that the Office of the Municipal Accountant (MA) is undermanned. Also, some documents were not attached to the vouchers resulting to delay on the submission of said disbursement.
observations.reason: The reason for the delay on the submission of the DVs and its attachments is that the Office of the Municipal Accountant (MA) is undermanned. Also, some documents were not attached to the vouchers resulting to delay on the submission of said disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Trial Balances not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month.
observations.managementAction: Some tasks are being delegated to Job Order personnel to fast-track submission of the Monthly Trial Balance.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accounting Office is undermanned which resulted to the delay in the submission of the monthly trial balances. However, the Municipal Accountant exerts his best effort to submit on or before the due date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the LCE to require the GSO-Designate to prepare and maintain the Property Cards for each PPE item and submit to COA for validation. We also recommend strict compliance with Section 119 of the Manual on the NGAS.
observations.reason: Audit observation was reiterated under Observation No. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instructs the MPDO to ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready. We also recommended that the LCE instructs the Municipal Engineer to speed-up the implementation of the projects under the 20% DF in order that the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes were attained during the year.
observations.reason: Similar finding is discussed in Observation No. 20, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instructs the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the MDRRM Officer to restore back to the LDRRMF from the General Fund Proper the amount of ₱117,000.00 to make the funds available for future use. Corresponding correction to the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) must also be made to adjust the amounts of expenditures under the General Fund Proper and the LDRRMF. We also recommended that the LCE requires the MDRRMC of strict compliance with Item 4.3 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.
observations.reason: Audit observation was reiterated under Observation No. 21.b, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRMO-Designate to strictly comply with Section 12 (c) (22) of RA No. 10121 and Item No. 7.2.8 of NDRRMC- DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01 with regards to implementation of the PPAs under the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Similar finding is discussed in Observation No. 21.e, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE requires the MA to transfer from the General Fund to the Trust Fund the unexpended/unobligated 30% QRF and the DRRMF- MOOE by following the accounting entries prescribed under Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Audit observation was reiterated under Observation No. 21.b, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE enjoined the MDRRMC to observe the procedures for the disposition of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF and also requires the Municipal Budget Officer, MA and the MDRRMO-Designate to comply with the reporting requirements for the LDRRMF in accordance with Items 4.4 and 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Similar finding is discussed in Observation No. 21.a, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE requires: (a) the concerned officials and employees to liquidate the outstanding balances of their CAs as of December 31, 2020 and to strictly enforce compliance with Sections 4.1.3 and 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 197-002; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already issued a Memorandum to all officials and employees to liquidate all cash advances as of December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Audit observation was reiterated under Observation No. 9, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to: (a) determine the complete details of the receivable accounts and thereafter communicate with the concerned accountable officers, debtors and recipient agencies for the settlement of the Receivables, hence, the accounts; and (b) prepare the Aging of Receivables and disclose the details of the Receivables in the Notes to FS. We also recommended that the LCE request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts if settlement thereof were not ascertained after exhausting all remedies by following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant can no longer determine the details of the Receivables, hence, aging of Receivables can no longer be prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the approval of the CLUP and revisit its CDP and ensure that climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction activities will be integrated in its land use plan.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already submitted the CLUP to DHSUD for approval.
observations.reason: The Plan was not yet approved by the Department of Human Settlements and Urban Development (DHSUD).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE secure legal documents as proof of ownership of the land where the agency structures were built. We also recommended that the LCE ensure that ownership of the lands is legally transferred to the LGU to avoid future legal actions from rightful owners.
observations.managementAction: There is no Municipal Assessor to help with the registration of titles. The Management assured that the only permanent staff in the Municipal Assessor’s Office, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant will start the registration of titles in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: There is no Municipal Assessor to help with the registration of titles.

observations.recommendation: 3. Make the necessary actions to file a request with COA for write-off of the dormant Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivables accounts in the total amount of ₱2.027 million in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to clean the books of accounts and arrive at a fair presentation of the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management filed for write-off but was disapproved and returned due to new COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

observations.recommendation: 4. Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost as a result of delayed implementation. Also, re-evaluate the necessity of the projects under the continuing appropriation and consider the realignment of insignificant projects.
observations.managementAction: Re-evaluation of projects was done by the Management but project implementation is still delayed.

observations.recommendation: 12. Constitute an Inspection Committee to determine and classify the unserviceable properties. The valueless should be destroyed in the presence of the Auditor under the supervision of the proper committee or if found valuable, it should be disposed of on the manner provided under Section 379 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The designated Property Custodian started inventory of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee and the GSO – Designate to conduct complete physical count of all PPE at least once a year, submit the RPCPPE that includes all properties and coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and

The Municipality undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Management revise its Municipal Revenue Code in order to strengthen the Municipality’s revenue generating capacity so that more funds will be available for the implementation of the government’s programs, projects and activities in accordance with RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Management:

Conduct proper planning by considering all planned activities for the year and anticipate occurrence of irregular activities to determine the adequate number of bonded personnel and the reasonable amount of fidelity bond to be applied for each accountable officer; and

Consider adopting an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system for the salaries and wages of municipal employees and job orders to reduce the number and amount of cash advances of the Administrative Officer V/Cashier as well as provide greater convenience, transparency and accountability.

We recommended the following:

Implement the projects funded by the fund transfers in order to provide the intended benefits to the public;


Review the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for the said fund transfers in order to determine if there are special provisions regarding unexpended balances; and

Return the unexpended balances of the fund transfers to the respective source agencies if there are no provisions provided in the MOA that such balances should be retained by the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund for socio-economic developments; and



Prepare a monthly utilization report detailing the status and timeframe of each project/program to facilitate in the monitoring of such fund.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and

The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Acting Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to various NGOs/POs in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2016-005.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of the FURs and the full liquidation of the fund transfers granted to the concerned barangays in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2016-005.

We recommended that the management immediately enforce the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances exceeding three years as of December 31, 2013 pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We also recommended that monthly deduction in the payroll of persons liable be effected to conform with COA policy regarding the settlement of disallowances through installment payment.  The payment period shall be for 36 months only. 


We further recommended that the accounting personnel exert effort to look into the difference between Receivable-Disallowances/charges account reflected in the financial statement and reports issued by Auditor such as RADC.  Henceforth, always reconcile the balance of the said account with RADC and SASDC issued by the auditor quarterly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the GSO-Designate to maintain records regarding acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information of PPE based on the source documents of the transactions and reconcile with the Accounting Records.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall update the record of acquisition/disposal /transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.

We further recommended that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant, plan/ strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for the purpose of one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts, and to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1 of this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. require all officials and employees to liquidate their outstanding CAs at
year-end; and
b. instruct the MA to refrain from allowing additional CAs to any official or
employee unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or a proper
accounting thereof is made and issue a certification that the previous cash
advance of the concerned accountable officer was already liquidated.

We recommended the management instruct the MA to abide strictly with the
prompt submission of CA DVs for CF in accordance with COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-
DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 08, 2015, to ensure timely review of
the transactions.

We recommended that Management:
a) instruct the MA to (i) make sure that all ongoing infrastructure projects are
accurately recorded as CIP in the FS as at year-end; and (ii) draw accounting
entries to correct the misstatements in CIP and AP accounts; and
b) ensure the commitment of the MEO to submit to the MAO a monthly
monitoring report/SWA regarding the status/progress of the construction of all
ongoing projects to facilitate the timely recording of CIP in the books.

We recommended that Management:
a. instruct the MA to (i) draw accounting entries to correct the misstatements in
Liabilities accounts:
(ii) ensure to account for the revenues from NGAs in the TF books by
recognizing Subsidy from the National Government after the fulfillment of the
conditions or purpose stipulated in the agreement pursuant to Sections 44 to
47 of IPSAS 23; and (iii) restate the Comparative Statement of Financial
Performance of the ensuing year to reflect the recognition of revenues and the
payment/accrual of expenses involving non-CO fund transfers; and
b. instruct the OIC-MT to promptly liquidate CA by the end of the year and
recognize the corresponding expenses associated with these advances in the
appropriate accounting period.

Nonetheless, we recommended that the (a) ME coordinate with the contractors to
comply with the regular updating of the project status to comply with the guidelines on
information and publicity on PPAs; and (b) the Bids and Awards Committee to
require the winning contractor to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer
to (i) include in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP/AIP the list of PPAs to be
undertaken out of the unexpended balance of the prior year LDRRMF to rationalize
the use of the Special Trust Fund; (ii) strive for the timely and full implementation of
LDRRM-related PPAs to achieve the goals and objective of the fund and the desired
benefits; and (iii) submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF
within the reglementary period and the Revised/Updated LDRRMFIP to COA Office
for timely review of the report.

We recommended that Management to:
a) instruct the MA to draw journal entries to correct misstatements pertaining
to the STF DRRMF; and
b) and henceforth, require the MA and the MBO to regularly review and
reconcile their respective records and registries and make the necessary
adjustments to correct the errors.

We recommended the Management to:
a) instruct the OIC-MT to undertake the following courses of action:
(i) regularly examine the books of accounts of business establishments
to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales or receipts
used in the computation of business taxes paid following Section 171
of R.A. No. 7160;
(ii) assign personnel at the MTO who are well-equipped with the
necessary knowledge and training in the examination of books of
accounts to enable the said Office to carry on with their mandate
efficiently; and
(iii) tag all the afore-cited suppliers and contractors with discrepancies
noted in declared sales or receipts and be subject to examination of
books of accounts to ensure the correctness of taxes, fees, and
charges to be collected;
b) advise the BPLO Chief to implement the following measures:
(i) continue to require the submission of complete documentation of
the financial records from taxpayers for the correct assessment of
business taxes;
(ii) prescribe a standard format of Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or
Receipts when applicable and require the business taxpayers to
have it duly notarized upon submission;
(iii) coordinate with the ME to identify the contractors who have
bunkhouses or other structures that may be considered as project
office as a basis for the imposition of business tax;
(iv) collect business taxes from outside contractors who engaged in
various businesses within the Municipality applying the situs of
taxation;
(v) strictly impose the 25% surcharge and 2% monthly interest on
delinquent business taxes;
(vi) submit a written explanation on the noted several cases of
inaccurate computations of surcharges and interests for proper
85
disposition by the AT; and
(vii) require the concerned taxpayers to pay the business tax deficiency
of at least ₱1,886,054.39; and
c) require the OIC-MT and the BPLO Chief to devise measures to validate
the declared gross sales or receipts of establishments applying for business
permits especially those who are having direct transactions with the
Municipality and its Barangays.

We recommended that Management instruct the GFPS to:
a) undertake a thorough review of the GAD expenditures to identify the specific
areas where expenditures exceeded budgetary limits and to analyze
contributing factors behind these occurrences;
b) attach the documents required under Paragraph 5 of Section 4.0 (C.8) of JMC
2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs
implemented during the year;
c) observe the deadline for submission of GAD AR to the AT;
d) administer the HGDG test whenever it uses attribution in the budgeting phase
and AR preparation; and
e) seek help from experts and include HGDG training in the budget to ensure the
correctness of the GAD issues identified and the responsiveness of the
corresponding GAD PPAs.

We recommended that management instruct the MA and the OIC-MT
including all AOs to (a) immediately transmit to the AT the unsubmitted accounts
and financial reports; and (b) strictly observe the timeframes for the submission of
accounts and financial reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We commended the Management particularly the MAO and the MTO for the
prompt remittance of the taxes withheld during the current year to the BIR. This
notwithstanding, we recommended that Management continuously take
responsibility to comply with the withholding and timely remittance of taxes in
conformity with RR No. 2-98 and RMC No. 23-2007 on the prompt remittance of the
taxes due to the BIR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA to:
a) carefully review the entries in the SLs and make the necessary adjusting
entries to correct the balances of affected accounts; and
b) henceforth, ensure to thoroughly analyze the journal entries made before
posting the same in the SLs to ensure accurate and complete recording of
transactions and reliable balances of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:

a. Prioritize the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as soon as possible
by reconstituting an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of
members who can be temporarily relieved from their regular duties and be able to
complete the physical inventory in three months or less;

b. Instruct the Inventory Committee, GSO and the Accounting Unit to perform the
preliminary activities by preparation of appropriate reports such as latest RPCPPE
and PPELCs and reconcile their records before the actual physical inventory; and

c. Direct the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO, to plan/strategize on
how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period
and thereafter, prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing, at the least, the
specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date and a
schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from
start up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory and submit to the COA
Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory
activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
recognize in the books the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of
Accounts Payable (AP) for goods received or services rendered, inspected and
accepted, and for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and
inspected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare
the financial statements in compliance with the requirements of IPSAS including the
required reconciliation of the actual amounts of income and expenses per Statement
of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) with the figures presented in
the Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Strictly record claims as Accounts Payable only and when complete supporting
documents are submitted to substantiate their validity; and,

b. Immediately require the submission of documents for all outstanding payables as
of January 31, 2024 and validate the claims based on valid and complete supporting
documents and/or proof of the actual delivery of goods and services. Otherwise,
effect necessary adjustments/reversal in the books to conform with regulations.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services
Officer to strictly ensure that documents evidencing delivery of goods and services,
such as Delivery Receipts, Statement of Account/Contractor’s Bill, as applicable, and
Inspection and Acceptance Reports along with the procurement documents are
submitted in support of claims for payment forwarded to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the balance to appropriate income account and record the subsequent 20%
share of the Municipality from the Fire Code Fees as Permit Fees in the General Fund
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009, RA 9514 and BLGF MC No. 03-01-2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly refrain from processing disbursement
vouchers for a new cash advance of an employee until his/her previous cash
advance has been settled;

b. Designate an adequate number of disbursing officers for cash advance who will
be able to handle numerous cash advances of the Municipality one at a time, or if
none is available, request for the creation of new positions in the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office to the Sangguniang Bayan; and,

c. Direct concerned personnel to liquidate cash advances issued to them within the
prescribed timeline indicated in COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Administrator to
countersign all the checks drawn by the Municipal Treasurer excluding those checks
charged against the SB budget pursuant to the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require concerned
contractors of ongoing infrastructure projects to submit their DOLE-approved CSHP in
compliance with DOLE DO No. 13, s. 1998 and Section 37.2.3(f) of the Revised IRR of
RA No. 9184.

We also recommended that the BAC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and Municipal Accountant to require the contractors to
submit CSHP duly approved by DOLE for future projects to be implemented by the
Municipality, and in case the contractor could not submit the approved CSHP, deduct
the corresponding CSHP cost from the payment to the contractor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal Development Council, to assess the feasibility of the implementation and
responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents of those unimplemented
projects and include development projects that are well-planned and procurement-
and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2020-01. Also, lessen frequent changes in the planned PPAs to ensure
immediate implementation and completion within the target date.

b. Municipal Engineer and MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant
regarding the status and cost incurred for each project implemented by the
Municipality, particularly at year-end. As the Head of the Requesting Unit, he shall
prepare the Obligation Request (OBr) and the Disbursement Voucher (DV) on a
timely basis and forward these, together with the appropriate supporting
documents, to the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant,
respectively;

c. Municipal Accountant to recognize the Accounts Payable and corresponding
Property, Plant and Equipment accounts for the unpaid balance of completed
infrastructure projects as of December 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to
implement the programs and projects funded under the LDRRMF, within
the intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of its benefits to the
constituents. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those
identified PPAs to be included in the LDRRMFIP are those that can be implemented
and completed within the target period;

b. Municipal Accountant to review the amount transferred to the Trust Liability -
DRRM account pertaining to the unexpended balances of prior year
appropriations. Likewise, prepare an adjusting entry through the issuance of
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if necessary and submit such copies to the audit
team;

c. MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of the
reported appropriation, utilization and balance of the LDRRM Funds and effect
necessary changes to their records, if any;

d. Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers per year of transfer of the
unexpended balance of DRRM Funds that were transferred to the Special Trust
Fund.; and,

e. MDRRMO to review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include the activities to
be funded from the unexpended QRF and Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (DRRM-MOOE) of the previous
years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the
affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts
due to these agencies and identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some
accounts and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary
financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties; and

b. Review the taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors and ensure
that these taxes are remitted on the prescribed period to avoid penalties and
surcharges.
We recommended that the LCE require the MA to prepare and submit to the AT a revised and corrected BRS for re-verification.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the:
1. MA and GSO-Designate to update regularly the Stock Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and reconcile balances of these records with the to be able to reflect a complete and accurate balances in the financial statements; and
2. IC to conduct physical count of Inventories and to prepare and submit the required reports to the AT on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.

We recommended the LCE to temporarily relieve the members of the IC of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed, ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and instruct the:

IC to
1. Prepare a PIP and submit the same to the AT for the subsequent physical count at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities, together with the Invitation Letter to witness the entire inventory taking, in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of said Circular;
2. Assign a unique PN for each of the PPE items of all PPE accounts of the LGU following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular;
3. Ensure that Property Stickers attached to PPE items show all vital information of the PPE as required under section 5.7 of the Circular;
4. Record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE, List of PPE Found at Station and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs.

GSO-Designate
5. Prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs after the physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.

IC, GSO-Designate and MA
6. Reconcile all records and reports pertaining to PPE after the one-time cleansing of PPE balances is successfully done and establish the correct balances of the PPE accounts to be recorded in the books of accounts; and
7. Review all documents and reports before submission to the AT to ensure completeness and accuracy of details presented thereon.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

the MA
1. In joint coordination with the Municipal Treasurer (MT) and MAssO, to retrieve and secure at least 50 per cent documents to be used as basis of valuation of the Land account in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 17;
2. To provide this office with basis for recording the Land account to ascertain whether valuation of these properties is proper and reasonable; and
3. To update the Real Property Ledger Card of all components of Land account based on its accurate valuation;

the GSO-Designate to
4. Facilitate preparation of documentary requirements to secure the TCT of untitled parcels of Land to support claims of ownership; and
5. Update property cards of all items under Land account indicating all necessary information; and

MA, GSO-Designate and MAssO to
6. Reconcile their records pertaining to Land account.

We recommended the LCE to direct the MA:
1. To further analyze the Subsidiary Ledgers for PPE and determine the semi-expendable items acquired and issued on CY 2022 and prior years. Thereafter, make the necessary adjusting entries to record the carrying amount to expense or Prior Period Adjustment, as the case may be, in the books of accounts; and
2. Coordinate with the GSO-Designate in implementing the new capitalization threshold of newly acquired PPE and other guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2022-004 in the issuance of semi-expendable items and its recording in the books of accounts and other records.
We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to submit the monthly BRS within the period prescribed in Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the LCE request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to open another bank account for the proper segregation of SEF and TF in compliance with Section 310 of R.A. No. 7160.

We also recommended for the LCE to require the MA to expedite the preparation of the BRS, as much as possible up to the real-time BRS, and book adjusting and correcting entries for all valid reconciling items duly verified by the AT.

Thereafter and in coordination with the MT, facilitate the transfer to another bank account any of the cash in bank balance of SEF and TF.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Require the AOs concerned to cause the immediate liquidation of the CAs totaling ₱150,267.43 through the submission of the required documentation to the AT, otherwise, this may constitute a ground for a fine not exceeding one thousand pesos or by imprisonment not exceeding six months, or both such fine and imprisonment in the discretion of the court pursuant to Sec. 128 of PD No. 1445;
2. Direct the MA to issue Certification that the previous CAs have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting additional CAs; and
3. Thereafter, require strict adherence to the provisions of Sec. 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Cir. No. 97- 002 and EO No. 298 and other rulings mentioned above.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the:
1. Disposal Committee (DC) to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the procedures set by COA-DBM JC No. 2024-01 dated January 30, 2024;
2. GSO-Designate to accomplish and update the IIRUP after disposal of waste materials and furnish copy to the DC and AT; and
3. MA, pending the disposal of unserviceable properties, to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements all unserviceable properties that were not yet disposed as of December 31, 2023 to present fairly the PPE in the FS.

We recommended the LCE to direct the MA in close coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking documents, depending on the nature of transaction on the purchase of land, as indicated in Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, free from any liens and encumbrances, to prove ownership thereof.

Henceforth, deeds to property purchased by the Municipality shall be evidenced by documents that the title is vested in the government.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the MA to:
1. Ensure completeness of the required documentation of the DVs to facilitate audit disposition; and
2. Strictly comply with Section 5.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005.

We recommended the LCE to direct the MA to:
1. Obtain the lacking OR from the contractor pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445, Section 237 (a) of National Internal Revenue Code of 1997 (NIRC) and Items 3.1 and 3.3.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2004-006; and
2. Ensure that DVs for infrastructure-related disbursements in favor of contractors be sufficiently and adequately attached with the mandatory supporting documents common to all infrastructure projects, OR in the name of LGU and CSHP duly received by DOLE pursuant to PD No. 1445, pertinent provisions of DOLE Department Order No. 13, series of 1998, Section 37.2.3(g) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE direct its concerned personnel to refrain from engaging the notarial services of private lawyers. Henceforth, in the absence of a MLO, the LGU may instead seek counsel from the PLO or may secure the services of the PP for necessary legal assistance and notarization of documents.

We recommended that the LCE ensure faithful compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to effectively control Fuel, Oil and Lubricants by implementing the following corrective measures:
1. DTTs prepared by the Drivers prior to use of LGU motor vehicles shall be in prescribed format and are properly filled out;
2. MROT and MRFC are accomplished for verification purposes; and
3. Exercise tighter control over the use of government vehicles.

4. Submit a corrected AIP per PPAs with a total of ₱3,579,696.00.

2. Submit waiver or letter from the concerned employees showing disinterest in the appropriated funds for terminal pay; and
3. Submit MDC Resolution declaring savings under the 20 per cent EDF and/or reason for the discontinuance of the original projects and approving the re-appropriation of same to new PPAs.

1. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and

We recommended the LCE to direct the:

LDRRMC to

2. Fully implement the PPAs as indicated in LDRRMF Plan; and
3. Optimize the utilization of its LDRRMF to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.

MBO in coordination with LDRRMC to
4. Restore back to the LDRRMF from the GF Proper the amount of ₱197,161.79 to make the funds available for future use;
5. Refrain from allocating PPAs which are not considered valid under the LDRRMF.

MA and MBO to

8. Record the purchase of welfare goods for distribution to inventory account and issuances thereof to the appropriate expense accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to ensure that income taxes are withheld from future payments made to all suppliers, contractors and service providers and the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.
