The Municipal Treasurer requires all revenue collecting officers to remit religiously their collections on a daily basis, or in the case of the collectors assigned in the field, where travel time from places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as their collections reach P5,000.00.
Accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I;
Collectors issue in chronological order the pre-numbered official receipts in strict numerical sequence; and
Management impose disciplinary actions or sanctions when necessary, in order to enforce compliance with the existing rules and regulations on collection, reporting and remittances and in order to safeguard government funds.

The LDRRMO review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund and submit the same to the audit team; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statement and accounting record the particular year to which the balances of STF-DRRM were unexpended for proper monitoring and budgeting and any unutilized balance aged more than five years shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

Direct the GAD Focal Person to expedite the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to appropriately identify gender issues and provide basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
Direct the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to conduct annual monitoring of the accomplishments and evaluation of the utilization and outcome of the GAD budget and prepare the form prescribed in Item H of the JMC No. 2013-01.

The Local Chief Executive ensure the timely submission of properly accomplished action plan on the CY 2023 AAR findings and recommendations including that of the prior years and submit the same to the audit team; and
Hereafter, the Management shall faithfully observe the 60-day period within which to submit to the audit team from receipt of the succeeding AARs, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee and the MGSO – Designate to include all types of properties in the physical count of PPE, submit the RPCPPE that includes all properties and coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and
The Municipality undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Determine an appropriate useful life and recognize depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for all PPEs, including those intended to be transferred to different barangays in the Municipality;
Review the computation of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for all depreciable PPE accounts following the prescribed procedures provided by IPSAS No. 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Management:
Submit copies of debit and credit memos that support transactions processed by the bank for verification in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Provide a sufficient description of the reconciling items on the face of the BRS or in any other supporting document or schedule to facilitate verification of the accuracy and legitimacy of the reconciling items.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that all necessary documents and required certifications for procurement be prepared and submitted in adherence to Section 86 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Management:
Assign a technically knowledgeable staff from the Municipal Engineering Office or from other departments to assist the MPDC in the preparation of the POW and other technical specifications of the PPAs under the 20% DF;
Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund for socio-economic developments; and
Thoroughly evaluate the proposed projects ensuring that all factors are considered prior to approval in order to minimize delays and reprogramming of PPAs.

We recommended that the Management:
Implement the projects funded by the fund transfers in order to provide the intended benefits to the public;
Review the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for the said fund transfers in order to determine if there are special provisions regarding unexpended balances; and
Return the unexpended balances of the fund transfers to the respective source agencies if there are no provisions provided in the MOA that such balances should be retained by the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to timely submit to the Audit Team the complete information on all ongoing government projects, programs and activities (PPA) as well as those that are to be implemented during the year as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the monthly project status reports of various government PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI Form on the audit observations and recommendations within sixty (60) days from receipt of the AAR to the Office of the Auditor. Moreover, all items in the form be accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor order the:
GSO-Designate to maintain and update Property Cards regarding acquisition, description, custody, disposal, and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions, and reconcile with the Accounting Records and RPCPPE.
Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant:
to complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts.
Prepare a report on the result of the physical count through RPCPPE including list of unserviceable PPEs, missing PPEs, and items found at station.
Any discrepancy between the RPCPPE, PPELCs, and PCs shall be investigated, verified, and adjusted. The total reconciled balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance of PPE at year-end; and;
to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Temporary relief of the personnel who will conduct the physical count from their daily task to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed within the deadline set.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the corresponding depreciation of the infrastructure assets from the date of completion as indicated in the Certificate of Completion, and for the machinery and equipment, from the date of delivery as indicated in the Acceptance and Inspection Report.  The depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant draw adjusting entries to recognize the depreciation of the PPE items not subjected to depreciation in CY 2023 and prior years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a)    The Supply Officer to:
    i)    prepare an Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for items costing below P15,000.00, and Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) for items costing P15,000.00 and above upon its issuance to end-users;
    ii)    use the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) to record all supplies issued;
    iii)    prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued based on the RIS; and
    iv)    submit the RSMI, duly supported with all RIS, to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts.
b)    The Municipal Accountant to:
    i)    reclassify the supplies for stockpiling from the Inventory Held for Consumption to the appropriate account under the Inventory Held for Distribution; and
    ii)    prepare the appropriate Journal Entry Voucher to record the issued supplies to the end-users based on the RSMI and RIS of the Supply Officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC Chairperson, undertake the following:
Require the submission of the latest Business Tax Return within five (5) calendar days from receipt by the bidder of the notice from the BAC that the bidder has the Lowest Calculated Bid or Highest Rated Bid.
Carefully verify, validate, and ascertain all statements made in the documents submitted by the bidder, and shall immediately notify the said bidder in writing of its post-disqualification and the grounds for it, if any, and only declare bidders as eligible if they strictly adhere to the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184.
Submit justification why the contractor was awarded with four (4) new contracts despite having negative slippage of its on-going and previous contracts with the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor impose liquidated damages of at least one-tenth (1/10) of one (1) percent of the cost of unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay, and ensure that the time extension granted is reasonable.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
determine the reasonableness of the contractor's requests for time extension by examining thoroughly the supporting relevant documents before recommending its approval to the Municipal Mayor.
Submit to the Audit Team supporting documents to justify the grant of time extension due to unfavorable weather conditions.
Provide for the computation of the additional liquidated damages to be imposed for the project “Repairs and maintenance of the Legislative Building” and Construction/Extension of Multi-purpose Drying Pavement at Brgy. Tigbao”
Monitor the performance of the contractors to ensure the timely implementation of infrastructure projects.
Include the pre-determined rainy/unworkable days in the computation of the original contract time during the conduct of a detailed engineering plan.
We finally recommended the Municipal Accountant make a demand letter for the collection of the liquidated damages not imposed on the contractor based on the computation provided by the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a)    Municipal Accountant to  disclose all relevant information pertaining the available, utilized and balances of the current year and Special Trust Fund of Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
b)    MDRRMO to ensure that all programs/projects/activities charged against all sources of the LDRRMF are incorporated in the LDRRMFIP or Revised LDRRMFIP.
c)    Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to prepare a supplemental Annual Investment Program based on the LDRRMFIP or Revised LDRRMFIP, and secure approval of the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the commencement of project implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a)    Require the deputy collectors to remit all collections daily and intact to the Municipal Treasurer; and
b)    Issue a written authority deputizing the Barangay Treasurers to collect Community Tax Certificate on her behalf.  In the absence of said authority, require the collectors to immediately turnover collections and accountable forms to the Municipal Treasurer.  Henceforth, no accountable forms shall be issued to Barangay Treasurers without a written authority as deputy collectors.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) that in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan, conduct a thorough review of the Peace and Order and Public Safety (POPS) Plan of the LGU and ensure that all proposed PPAs are aligned with the priorities outlined in the POPS Plan and duly supported by the Peace and Order Plan.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Peace and Order Councils to include only those PPAs that are directly related to crime prevention and law enforcement activities, capability development for personnel of law enforcement agencies, volunteers/partners, or programs for anti-illegal drug, illegal gambling, counter-insurgency, and/or counter-terrorism efforts, among others that will directly contribute to the maintenance of the peace and order and public safety.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Health Officer:
i)    issue a Special Order covering the assignment of public health workers to hazardous/difficult areas,
ii)    secure Certification by the Secretary of the DOH that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-torn/embattled/disease-infested/distress or isolated areas/stations, or areas declared under state of calamity or emergency, or with volcano activity or eruption, and
iii)    attach the complete supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers.
We likewise recommended the Municipal Accountant certify disbursement vouchers that are supported with complete documentation and strictly observe the guidelines on the grant of hazard pay, subsistence and laundry allowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request for write-off any dormant fund transfers, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.  Also, direct:
The Municipal Accountant to:
a)    Make representation with the source agencies to reconcile unliquidated fund transfers between the source and implementing agencies books and prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted.
b)    Prepare the appropriate journal entries to reclassify awards and LGU share of Fire Code Fees received.
c)    Ensure that liquidation reports are duly supported with the required documentation provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001.
The Municipal Treasurer to:
a)    refund/remit to the source agencies or the Bureau of Treasury the unexpended balance after the completion of the purpose of fund transfers.
b)    Submit to the Municipal Accountant the proof of receipt of fund transfers and refund of unexpended balance for its attachment to the liquidation report.

We commended the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned in ensuring the full implementation of all the priority PPAs under the 20%DF as embodied in the AIP and the optimal utilization of the funds in compliance with the provisions of Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020.
We recommended the Management continually make a conscious effort to accomplish the identified development projects efficiently and effectively in accordance with rules and regulations.
We further recommended the Municipal Budget Officer, to effect the reversion of the savings from the continuing appropriations and capital outlay to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund through a resolution before the re-appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MHO to:
a. provide justification/explanation, together with HCWs, corroboratory and
substantial reports and documents to support the same, relative to the
discrepancies noted in the reporting of the COVID-19 Vaccination Program;
b. submit the pertinent documents/records to the GSO and MAO to facilitate the
evaluation of the receipts and issuances/utilization of COVID-19
vaccines/boosters and ancillary supplies and the recording of the remaining
balances of unused boosters inventories in the books and property records;
c. coordinate with the PHO to secure the documents that contain the costs of
the items donated as needed for the recording thereof; and
d. ensure to align the inventory procedures with the existing rules and
regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned HCWs/ Non-HCWs were able to submit the required
documents; Pertinent documents/ records were submitted to the GSO and MAO
to facilitate the evaluation of the receipts and utilization of the COVID-19
vaccines/ items donated cost was made. Alignment of the inventory
procedures with the existing rules and regulations was ensured.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. LHB and the concerned officials to submit a written explanation on (i) the
non-certification of the list of public HCWs and non-HCWs who were eligible to
receive OCA; and (ii) the internal arrangement of sharing the OCA among HCWs
and non-HCWs who were not on the official list submitted to the DOH and the
reason for not including the said personnel to the list submitted if they were indeed
eligible to receive the benefits;
b. concerned HCWs/non-HCWs to provide valid proofs that they had physically
reported for work and had provided direct health care support as well as the actual
and detailed ARs relative thereto; and
c. MHO or concerned supervisor, being the head of the health facility, to ensure
that ARs involved in COVID-19 response are in line with the prevention,
detection, isolation, treatment, rehabilitation, and vaccination strategy.
observations.managementAction:
Written explanation on the non-certification of public HCWs and non-HCWs
and the internal arrangement of sharing the OCA among HCWs and non-HCWs
were submitted to AT. The concerned HCWs/Non-HCWs were able to submit the
required documents; ARs involved in COVID-19 response were insured to be in
line with the prevention, detection, isolation, treatment, rehabilitation, and
vaccination strategy.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the HRMO to (i) prepare and issue policy guidelines to address the
issues on the necessity and propriety of hiring casual and JO personnel in order to
limit expenditures to programs/projects where their services are actually needed;
(ii) avoid the hiring of casual and JO personnel whose functions overlap or
duplicate those of the regular employees of the Municipality; (iii) Not hire JO
personnel based on necessity and skills/competencies specifically required in the
local projects; and (iv) re-evaluate the personnel complements to fill-up the
vacancies; and to fill-up the vacancies.
b. instruct the MAO to use DV in all disbursements involving ADA to ensure that
all ADA payments of the Municipality were duly approved in accordance with the
procedural requirements provided under existing laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Approved policy guidelines on the screening and hiring requirement of JOs
were issued. Concerned JOs had a personal appearance at AT for a head count.
Some JOs still performed the functions of regular employees. Re-evaluation of
the personnel complements to fill-up the vacancies were made. The MAO used
DV in all disbursements involving ADA to ensure that all payments of the
Municipality were duly approved in accordance with procedural
requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMC to:
a. properly plan for the inclusion/integration of activities to be funded from the
unexpended LDRRMF balances into the LDRRMFIP based on an extensive
evaluation of the vulnerability of the Municipality to natural or man-made
disaster risks to rationalize the use of the STF;
b. henceforth ensure that the LDRRMF is optimally utilized; and
c. sustain the current effective responses to disasters and its attendant risks.
observations.managementAction:
The unexpended balance from CY 2022 was not included in the LDRRMFIP.
The procurement and distribution of relief goods were efficiently conducted to
aid households affected by the seasonal monsoon, known as Habagat, and the
subsequent typhoon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer including all Accountable Officers to:
a. immediately transmit to the Audit Team the unsubmitted accounts and
financial reports; and
b. strictly observe the timeframes for the submission of accounts and financial
reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction:
Accounts and financial reports were submitted to AT. Minimal delays were
still incurred in the submission of accounts this year.
observations.reason: Minimal delays were still incurred in the submission of accounts this year.

observations.recommendation:

a. carefully review the entries in the SLs and make the necessary adjusting
entries to correct the balances of affected accounts; and
b. henceforth, ensure to thoroughly analyze the journal entries made before
posting the same in the SLs to ensure accurate and complete recording of
transactions and reliable balances of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Not Review of the entries in the SLs was conducted, but the required
adjustments were not executed in the affected accounts. Journal entries are
thoroughly analyzed before posting the same in the SLs.
observations.reason: Review of the entries in the SLs was conducted, but the required adjustments were not executed in the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation:

b. update the GAD database to include sex-disaggregated data;
c. administer the HGDG test whenever it uses attribution in the budgeting phase
and AR preparation; and
d. seek help from experts and include HGDG training in the budget to ensure the
correctness of the GAD issues identified and the responsiveness of the
corresponding GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Some GAD PPAs remained un-implemented as of year-end; the GAD Focal
Person has commenced the training on HGDG.

Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries relative to the prior years which were not included in the reclassification conform with the new capitalization threshold prescribed in COA Circular No. 2022-004 and for the proper classification of equipment accounts from Office of the Municipal General Services Officer to Information and Communication Technology Equipment account.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken.
The necessary adjustments were made in the books in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:

Municipal Treasurer to submit annually to the Municipal Accountant the list of all the taxpayers and their corresponding balances and submit the submitted report of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis for the correcting entries for the RPT and SET Receivable balances as of the beginning of CY 2022; and
b. Municipal Accountant to record the RPT and SET receivables annually based on the submitted report of the Municipal Treasurer and reconcile the outstanding balance of receivables with the amount of delinquencies in the records of the Municipal Treasurer.

The Municipal Accountant has recognized the RPT and SET receivables for CY 2023 based on the submitted report of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Department Heads to program the partially utilized or unutilized balances under the accounts Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs and Due to Other Funds (NFA) amounting to P2,181,608.54, P240,200.00, and P30,000.00, respectively, to the programs/projects as indicated in the purpose of the grant.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:

Determine the nature of the share from RA 8048 and share from collection of Building Permit amounting to P11,160.00 and P306,258.28, respectively, if these were legitimate share of the Municipality transfer the same to the GF of the Municipality, otherwise remit the same together with the balances of funds received for completed projects to the concerned National Government Agencies (NGAs) or to the National Treasury, in case the NGA was not identified, in accordance with COA Circular 94-013.
For the Other Payables account totaling P1,136,908.54, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor refer to the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-001 that in case it is not provided in the Memorandum of Trust/ Agreement/ Understanding, a written approval of the source agency must be secured to transfer the unexpended balances to the General Fund of the agency.

For the Other Payables account totaling P1,136,908.54, the management stated that the accounts were submitted. This was not implemented.

No reason was provided.
Other Payables - was provided.
This was not prioritized by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. MDRRM Officer and the MPD Officer to utilize the funds currently appropriated and those transferred to the Trust Liability – DRRM account. Conduct thorough planning and analysis to ensure that those identified PPAs in the LDRRMFIP include those programs and projects that are sourced from Trust Liability; b. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to present the correct year-end balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality. Henceforth, record under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account the DRRM funds received from other government agencies; c. Municipal Accountant to provide Subsidiary Ledgers per year of transfer of the unexpended balance of DRRM Funds that were transferred to the Trust Liability Account to fully identify which of the balances are over five years and due for transfer to the General Fund for programming under social services. Also transfer the SL of those fund transfers from other agencies intended for disaster response to the SL of the Trust Liability – DRRMF account; and d. Municipal Accountant to review the amount transferred to the Trust Liability - DRRM account pertaining to the unexpended balances and prepare an adjusting entry, if necessary and submit such copies to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
Complied as recommended by the Audit Team.
The CY 2022 unexpended balance amounting to P4,242,502.56 was transferred to the Trust Liability - DRRM account on February 2023.
observations.reason:
No reason was provided.
The Municipality implemented adjustments but some records remain to be reconciled.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairman, members and the Technical Working Group (TWG) of the Bids to: a. Scrutinize the documents submitted by each bidder as to validity and propriety during the bid evaluation and post-qualification process before awarding the contract to the winning bidder. Likewise, ensure submission by the bidder of the construction schedule, manpower schedule, equipment utilization schedule and safety and health program; and b. Prepare the necessary bidding documents noted in the observation for its CY 2023 procurement through public bidding. Likewise, ensure that the Certification of Posting at conspicuous places were made by the BAC Secretariat and posting of the Notice of Award, Contracts, and Notice to Proceed are posted in the PhilGEPS. Further, direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the posted Performance and Warranty Security for infrastructure projects remained valid in accordance with the prescribed period indicated in Section 62 of the IRR. Likewise, issue Certificate of Final Acceptance, in addition to the Certificate of Acceptance, one year after completion of projects.
observations.managementAction: To follow COA’s recommended actions, the Municipal Budget Officer had prepared the required bidding documents and posting requirements. The GSO submitted explanation on delays in delivery of various procurement of equipment which necessitates issuance of suspension orders. The necessary posting and certification were complied by the BAC. The Municipal Engineer ensured that the posted Warranty Security for the procurement of goods is in the form prescribed in Section 62 of the IRR.
observations.reason:
No declaration as Economic Enterprise was provided.
The Municipality implemented many recommendations starting CY 2024 but some procurement documentation issues remain from CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal General Services Officer take the following courses of action: a. Ensure that all radio communication permits were owned/used by the Municipality have pertinent permits such as Permit to Possess, Permit for base/mobile station, Radio Station License or Permit to Operate in compliance with the NTC Radio Law; and b. Secure LGU’s Permit to Purchase and supplier’s permit to sell before purchasing radio communication equipment and furnish the COA Audit Team with copy of said permits.
observations.managementAction: The necessary permits were required in subsequent purchase of radio communication equipment. The permits were secured by the Municipality. The permits were complied by the contractors. The GSO submitted explanation on the cause of delay in delivery of procurement of equipment, reagents and kits for COVID-19 testing which necessitated suspension orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer to take the necessary actions for the immediate transfer of ownership to the Municipality and secure Certificate of Title of the aforementioned land. Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer regarding fund requirements for the transfer/titling of the land in the succeeding Annual/Supplemental Budgets, if warranted; and b. Municipal Accountant to secure copies of those documents lacking on the procurement of land and submit it to the Audit Team, once available.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer has already processed the transfer of ownership and waiting for the release of Title from the Registry of Deeds. The necessary documents were submitted subsequently and the Register of Deeds for the approval of Deed of Conveyance, ECAR Authority to Register Deed from the DAR are in process.
observations.reason: Delay on the approval of the transfer of lot was attributed to the release of the registration of the property such as the approved plan which was required for the transfer. The Municipality reported that documentation is in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Consider the adoption of the procedures and guidelines on the one-time cleansing of PPE prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006; and b. Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to effect the adjustments and reclassification of capitalized items in prior years which were not included in the reclassification and update the records accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality of Ibaan created an inventory committee during the year but One-Time Cleansing was not yet adopted. The General Services Officer submitted the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and has commenced preliminary activities. The necessary adjustments were implemented in CY 2023.
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strongly require the Office of the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and MDRRMO to reconcile records as regards the above noted deficiencies. Hereafter, ensure that succeeding reports were already tally with other office’s record to present fairly the related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices conducted reconciliation. The current 2023 LDRRMP was prepared and approved by the MDRRMC and submitted to the Office of the MPDC for review and inclusion for the preparation of the Annual Investment Program. Transfers were made to the Trust Liability - DRRM account for prior year unexpended balances. Subsidiary Ledgers per year of transfer were established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Engineer, MPDC and MDRRMO to implement the planned projects/programs/activities on time.
observations.managementAction: The planned implemented projects/programs/activities for CY 2021 and 2022 were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council to: a. Assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects; and b. Ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20% Development Fund in the succeeding period by including the development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had implemented various PPAs funded from continuing appropriations of previous years and included projects in CY 2022 under 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Ensure procurement compliance; b. Secure necessary permits; c. Prepare appropriate documentation in future purchases.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality secured permits for subsequent procurements and complied with required documentation. The GSO provided explanations on delays and compliance issues which necessitated suspension orders where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited regulations; b. General Services Office to prepare and maintain Road Networks property cards and to submit Report on Local Road Network; c. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s Road network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was to prepare property ledger cards. The GSO submitted partial reports and the Municipal Engineer provided some project information. Inventory was partially conducted; some reconciliations pending due to pandemic restrictions and pending submissions from other agencies.
observations.reason: Data coming from other sources such as DPWH and PEO failed to supply. The pandemic restricts the fieldwork of the assigned personnel and employees to conduct the inventory count. Some documents were not submitted for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to refund to the municipal officials and employees the amount granted as P10,000.00 each, despite disapproval of Supplemental Budget by the Provincial Local Finance Committee as enacted by Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution/Ordinance No. 155 Year 2020.
observations.managementAction: To refund the amount of P1,104,000.00 granted as P1,104,000.00 was refunded by concerned municipal officials with the amount of P1,164,000.00 less the remaining P60,000.00 for the six deceased personnel. Copies of death certificates were attached in the submitted copy of official receipt. The Municipal Treasurer processed refunds for those who have refunded; the balance P60,000.00 was not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Prioritize and undertake the necessary action to complete the requirements needed in the approval of the 10 -year Solid Waste Management Plan as prescribed by R.A 9003; and b. Ensure compliance with related requirements and submit the plan for approval.
observations.managementAction: The 10 Year Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) has been reviewed by the MENRO Solid Waste team together with the PENRO and Regional EMB on August 18, 2023 for review and comments. The municipality complied with their recommendations for revision and submitted again and is waiting for final schedule of deliberation this December 2024.
observations.reason: The 10-year SWMP has not yet been approved by DENR-EMB; pending final deliberation and approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive Committee together with the Gender Focal Point System and the Technical Working Group: a. Conduct gender analysis during the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) Tool; and b. Establish and maintain GAD Database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01; and c. Ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all departments/offices of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Conducted initial orientation on HGDG attended by department heads in CY 2023. GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2025 was submitted and endorsed. GAD Database is being established through CBMS lodged at MPDC. Project proposals of different departments being reviewed by Municipal Administrator/GAD Focal Person to ensure integration of GAD concepts. Barangay GAD Plans and Budgets for 2024 and 2025 being reviewed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to pursue the titling of the 14 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership over the said property. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the circumstance as to the mode of acquisition of the land not covered with TCT so as not to mislead users of financial statements and appropriate valuation be taken up in the books. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Preparation and submission of requirements for transfers to agencies such as BIR, DAR, Register of Deeds, and others have been successfully completed for four (4) out of the 14 parcels of land owned by the Municipality. Consequently, the titles for these four (4) parcels have been released and are currently in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer; two (2) parcels are still in process, eight (8) properties, seven (7) are school sites and their titling process were deferred for further evaluation; while one (1) of the properties mentioned is a school building that will not be titled. Continuous coordination and follow up has been made with the agencies involved to complete the process. The Municipal Accountant is to prepare the necessary journal entries once documents are available.
observations.reason: Preparation and submission of requirements were completed for four parcels; others are in process or deferred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MAO to expedite the preparation of the BRS for all depository accounts of the Municipality and furnish copy to the Auditor. Accordingly, reconciling items should be booked to arrive at the correct Cash in Bank balance. 1.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct a thorough review of the

the differences in the account balances; (ii) derecognize in the books of
accounts the supplies and items which were already consumed and

entries to correct the misstatement of the Inventories accounts:
33
b. General Services Officer and other officials/personnel concerned to
prepare and submit on time the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) for the immediate recording of inventory issuances or
consumption for a given period; and

inventories; (ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count
of Inventories and submit the same to the Accounting Office for
reconciliation with the reported inventory balances in the books and to
the Office of the Auditor for verification at least once a year, if the
semestral reporting is not feasible.

a) Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to reconcile the
accounts for newly acquired PPEs;
b) Municipal Accountant to (1) draw JEVs taking up the (i) reversal of
entries made to record the on-going infrastructure projects without
corresponding Statement of Work Accomplishments from the
contractors validated by the Municipal Engineer; (ii) record the costs
of the on-going infrastructure projects; (iii) reclassification of the assets
acquired for distribution and small tangible items to the appropriate
inventory/expense accounts; (iv) reclassification of procured Motor
Cycle Sidecar (Kulong-Kulong) recorded as Other PPE to Motor
Vehicle Account; and (2) continue to update the PPELC for all property
accounts to support the recorded balances of PPE;
c) General Service Officer to (i) maintain complete and updated property
cards per category of PPE; and (ii) report all obsolete and unserviceable
PPE in the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) duly signed by the Head of Accounting Unit,
Inspector/Appraisal Committee and approved by Head of Agency or
authorized representative, and submit the report to the Disposal
Committee for proper disposition;
d) Municipal Budget Officer to recognize the obligation of the construction
of infrastructure projects based on progress billings and
accomplishment reports submitted by the contractors;
e) Inventory Committee to (i) conduct complete physical count of PPE;
and (ii) prepare/submit the corresponding RPCPPE (with appropriate

PPE Accounts Balances of the Municipality in compliance with COA
Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

recording in the books of account all undistributed items from in-kind

reporting guidelines of cash donations and in-kind donations; and
e) advise the LDRRMO to copy furnished the Audit Team of the report
submitted to the NDRRMC through the Office of Civil Defense (OCD).

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of the Municipality the Interest Expenses and Interest Payable accounts in consonance with accrual accounting and for purposes of fair presentation in the Agency’s financial statements.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the receivables from the unpaid space rentals to ensure accuracy and reliability of the Agency’s Financial Statements.

We recommended and management agreed to stop the practice of designating non-permanent employee to a supervisory position or job suited only for permanent or regular employee. Likewise, ensure that works performed by either casual or job order employees are adequately supervised by requiring proofs of work accomplishment or concrete outputs to eliminate problem on inefficiency and lack of accountability.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to immediately transmit the unsubmitted monthly accounts and financial reports; and (b) strictly adhere to the prescribed timeframes on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submittal of financial reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, we shall be constrained to enforce the provision of Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 on the withholding of salaries of all Municipal Officials deemed responsible for late/non-reporting of agency accounts.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and the Local Chief Executive through the MDRRMO agreed to ensure that the execution of projects designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as those projects pertaining to disaster prevention/mitigation and disaster preparedness, be given utmost priority.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations and management agreed to:
Abide strictly with the project schedule to ensure the full implementation of all the priority projects targeted for the year for the benefit of the Municipality and the constituents in general;
Consider the equitable distribution of PPAs listed among the three important sectors of development as prescribed under DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and previous issuances, and as mandated by Sections 16 and 17 of the Local Government Code (LGC) of 1991, to be able to cater not only the infrastructure needs of the constituents but also other concerns such as environment, agriculture, fishery and livelihood programs, projects and activities which will directly address the actual needs of the intended beneficiaries; and
Adopt well-defined strategies in order to enhance the present monitoring and review activities during the implementation and program evaluation phase, in order for the planners, implementers, as well as evaluators to continuously monitor the timely implementation of PPAs, and so that immediate appropriate and corrective actions could be done on noted gaps.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to undertake the following courses of action:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to post the Invitation to Bid and other Notices in the PhilGEPS and in the Agency website to promote transparency and competitiveness in all aspects of procurement; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the completeness of supporting documents of claims before releasing the same for payment.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to fulfill faithfully their obligation to remit the barangay shares from RPT collections within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271 (d) of R.A. No. 7160.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of Barangay Officials to support further the solid waste management program of the government by continuously educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;
Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board Member to monitor the implementation of “no segregation, no collection” policy within the Municipality; and
Provide solid waste containers with appropriate markings for recyclable, non-recyclable, compostable and special waste for on-site collection to facilitate waste segregation.

We recommended that management undertake the following remedial measures and courses of actions:

Require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to (i) provide clear and explicit information on the amount of appropriations approved for Peace and Order Program (POP) under the Annual Budget to support the allocation of funds for confidential expenses of the Municipality; and (ii) submit an excerpt or portion of the approved Annual Budget containing the programs/projects/activities to facilitate cross-referencing with the Statement of Itemized POP prepared by the Municipal Peace and Order Council (MPOC) Head Secretariat;

The Property Officer (i) to evaluate the necessity of repair or replacement and the need to adjust the angle of CCTV installation for better visibility; (ii) tap the technical assistance of the Engineering Office personnel, (iii) consider further the evaluation of the CCTV technical specifications considering the cost effectiveness, performance efficiency and effectiveness of the CCTV for optimum utilization; and (iv) consider installing a CCTV Monitoring Unit at the TMG Office near the highway, and all other major thoroughfares, as a tool in providing immediate assistance to public; and
Municipal Human Resource Management Officer and the Property Officer consider the necessity of hiring additional traffic personnel to man critical or accident-prone areas and provide the necessary communication equipment, supplies and facilities for better information dissemination.

14.3	We recommended that the management cause the immediate settlement of the balance of ₱33,571.20 and inform persons liable of the repercussions pursuant to the Revised Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA), in the case of failure to do so.

observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit some of the un-submitted supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Thorough feasibility study relative to the acquisition of loan was not made; hence, the availment thereof from a private bank could not be justified.

The issues noted by the Audit Team regarding the loan are still under discussion/ deliberation between the Bank and the management; hence, as of this writing, written agreement could not yet be produced.

Management allegedly made representation with the management of Eastwest Bank, however, issues as regards the guaranty clause were still unclear.

observations.recommendation:

a) exert utmost effort to compel the Project Proponent to faithfully fulfill its obligations provided in the Addendum of the MOA otherwise, institute legal remedy, if warranted; 

b) require the Project Proponent to post a performance security valid until issuance by the procuring entity of the final Certificate of Acceptance; and 

c) observe proper coordination with the Project Proponent for the unresolved problems encountered by the end-users to utilize the installed system in the preparation of financial and other reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that appropriation is available before obligations are incurred. Also, comply with the modified accrual basis of accounting for complete reporting of all financial transactions. Obligate all valid claims not paid during the year for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the obligation of Accounts Payable as at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management stop the hiring legal consultant except in those highly exceptional cases provided under paragraph 3(1), Section 481 of the Local Government Code of 1991. 

Likewise, please submit the following documents which were stated in the accomplishment reports submitted by the Consultant on Legal Affairs to support his claims to enable us to make a decision in audit:

result of legal researches and copy of opinions provided on matters referred to by the municipality; and

documents of the LGU notarized by the Consultant on Legal Affairs.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the hiring of legal consultants.
Management stopped the hiring of consultants that have no technical or professional expertise and with no definite expected outputs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely necessary interventions by the implementing offices may be made for their completion; (b) ensure that developmental projects for inclusion in the 20% Development Fund have all gone thorough study and priority ranking including coordination with other agencies to avoid conflicts and sudden change of strategy midstream; and (c) refrain from appropriating and/or utilizing funds for programs/projects/ activities (PPAs) under the 20% Development Fund which are not capital in nature.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to maximize the utilization of 20% DF.
observations.reason:
Only two projects were not implemented as of year-end. One was continued on the following year while the other one remained unimplemented due to some constraints with the Barangay.

One project was funded but not implemented due to the fact that it was not capital in nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the erroneous recording of equipment to their proper account; (b) Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; (c) concerned personnel to submit written explanation on the fabricated recipients of commodities and consider filing administrative and criminal actions against the personnel involved in the falsification of documents in violations of Art. 171 of the RPC; and (d) refund the amount of commodities supposedly issued to scholars totalling ₱72,495.50.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to reclassify the erroneous recording.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board observe prudence and diligence in giving priority to the programs and projects aligned with the purposes for which the SEF was established in accordance with Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447, Section 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991, Department of Education Culture and Sports (DECS) – Department of Budget and Management (DBM) – Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) Joint Circular Nos. 01 – B dated June 25, 2001, 01 – A dated March 14, 2000 and 01 dated April 14, 1998.

Furthermore, refrain from charging expenses for local travel, training, office supplies, electricity, other maintenance and operating expenses, purchase of desktop, laptop and printers for school heads and district office, internet connections, and the like to the Special Education Fund, otherwise, audit disallowance will be issued to require the restitution of the said amounts.
observations.managementAction: Management minimized spending on non- priority expenditures out of the SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

prioritize the immediate completion of Solid Waste Management Plan, submit the same to the NSWMC and have it reviewed and updated every year by the ESWMB;

reconsider and reevaluate the long idle property intended for the waste facility, when proved not applicable, address the need of an established waste facility; and

effect immediately the closure order issued by the DENR the soonest possible time unless the approval for the requested extension is sought.
observations.managementAction: Management resorted to operate a new open dumpsite after closure of the old one.
observations.reason:
The conduct of local elections hindered the management from focusing and prioritizing the use of the idle property as waste facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
